summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates path traversal by writing a generated file outside the intended output directory using ../../../ sequences in the output path.
expected_security_impact: Orval should write the generated endpoints.ts file to /tmp/ outside the intended /poc-output directory when the output path contains ../../../tmp/traversal-endpoints.ts.
actual_observed_result: File was successfully written to /tmp/traversal-endpoints.ts, proving that path traversal sequences in the -o argument are not validated and allow writing to arbitrary filesystem locations.
verification_method: The verification script checks if /tmp/traversal-endpoints.ts exists, confirming the file was written outside the intended workspace. This proves the path traversal vulnerability enables arbitrary file write.
evidence_produced_by_run: Generated file exists at /tmp/traversal-endpoints.ts outside the /poc-output workspace.
Path traversal sequence ../../../ was not blocked or sanitized.
No workspace boundary validation exists in the codebase.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out HEAD, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
